description: Jane Harbottle was appointed Chief Executive of the Legal Aid Agency (LAA) and Director of Legal Aid Casework in March 2021. Earlier in her career Jane conducted fraud investigations, litigation and debt recovery in the private sector. She joined the Legal Services Commission (the LAA’s predecessor organisation) as a contract manager in May 2000. Previous roles include: Interim Chief Executive Head of Civil Legal Aid and Head of Criminal Casework National Contracts Manager Senior Direct Services Contract Manager Area Contract Manager (North East) Litigation Manager

details.body:
Jane Harbottle was appointed Chief Executive of the Legal Aid Agency (LAA) and Director of Legal Aid Casework in March 2021.

Earlier in her career Jane conducted fraud investigations, litigation and debt recovery in the private sector. She joined the Legal Services Commission (the LAA’s predecessor organisation) as a contract manager in May 2000. Previous roles include:

  • Interim Chief Executive
  • Head of Civil Legal Aid and Head of Criminal Casework
  • National Contracts Manager
  • Senior Direct Services Contract Manager
  • Area Contract Manager (North East)
  • Litigation Manager

Jane Harbottle was appointed Chief Executive of the Legal Aid Agency (LAA) and Director of Legal Aid Casework in March 2021.

Earlier in her career Jane conducted fraud investigations, litigation and debt recovery in the private sector. She joined the Legal Services Commission (the LAA’s predecessor organisation) as a contract manager in May 2000. Previous roles include:

  • Interim Chief Executive
  • Head of Civil Legal Aid and Head of Criminal Casework
  • National Contracts Manager
  • Senior Direct Services Contract Manager
  • Area Contract Manager (North East)
  • Litigation Manager

The chief executive provides overall leadership of the LAA and works to ensure that legal aid is provided effectively and efficiently in England and Wales. Responsibilities include:

  • Legal aid case management
  • Legal aid commissioning and strategy
  • Legal aid finance and digital
  • LAA corporate central functions

Jane Harbottle was appointed Chief Executive of the Legal Aid Agency (LAA) and Director of Legal Aid Casework in March 2021.

Earlier in her career Jane conducted fraud investigations, litigation and debt recovery in the private sector. She joined the Legal Services Commission (the LAA’s predecessor organisation) as a contract manager in May 2000. Previous roles include:

  • Interim Chief Executive
  • Head of Civil Legal Aid and Head of Criminal Casework
  • National Contracts Manager
  • Senior Direct Services Contract Manager
  • Area Contract Manager (North East)
  • Litigation Manager
The chief executive provides overall leadership of the LAA and works to ensure that legal aid is provided effectively and efficiently in England and Wales.

Jane Harbottle was appointed Chief Executive of the Legal Aid Agency (LAA) and Director of Legal Aid Casework in March 2021.

Earlier in her career Jane conducted fraud investigations, litigation and debt recovery in the private sector. She joined the Legal Services Commission (the LAA’s predecessor organisation) as a contract manager in May 2000. Previous roles include:

  • Interim Chief Executive
  • Head of Civil Legal Aid and Head of Criminal Casework
  • National Contracts Manager
  • Senior Direct Services Contract Manager
  • Area Contract Manager (North East)
  • Litigation Manager

The Head of Criminal Casework leads the teams that process all applications and bills for criminal legal aid and central funds. This also includes the collection and enforcement of contributions made under the Crown Court means testing scheme.

The post-holder is a member of the Executive Leadership Team, chairs the LAA’s Operational Committee and has membership of the LAA board.

role_appointments.title: Jane Harbottle - Head of Criminal Casework

details.body:
Jane Harbottle was appointed Chief Executive of the Legal Aid Agency (LAA) and Director of Legal Aid Casework in March 2021.

Earlier in her career Jane conducted fraud investigations, litigation and debt recovery in the private sector. She joined the Legal Services Commission (the LAA’s predecessor organisation) as a contract manager in May 2000. Previous roles include:

  • Interim Chief Executive
  • Head of Civil Legal Aid and Head of Criminal Casework
  • National Contracts Manager
  • Senior Direct Services Contract Manager
  • Area Contract Manager (North East)
  • Litigation Manager

The Head of Civil Legal Aid is responsible for ensuring civil certificated applications and bills are processed effectively.

The post-holder leads teams processing civil applications, bills, payments on account, customer services, complaints handling, stakeholder training in civil processes, administration and the operation of the LAA national filing centre.

role_appointments.title: Jane Harbottle - Head of Civil Legal Aid

title: Jane Harbottle CBE
